openmeter/entitlement/driver/v2.purpose: V2 HTTP driver for the entitlement domain — adapts entitlement.Service and meteredentitlement.Connector to customer-scoped HTTP handlers via httptransport.HandlerWithArgs, always resolving a customer (by ID or key) before any entitlement operation. Differs from v1 by returning customerId/customerKey in all responses.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs typed triplet
patterns.description: Every endpoint declares Request, Response, Params type aliases and aliases the handler type as httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Req, Resp, Params]; the entitlementHandler method returns that alias.

patterns.name: Customer resolution before entitlement ops
patterns.description: Each handler resolves the customer via h.customerService.GetCustomer with CustomerIDOrKey, then checks cus.IsDeleted() returning models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError before any entitlement call.
patterns.example: cus, err := h.customerService.GetCustomer(ctx, customer.GetCustomerInput{CustomerIDOrKey: &customer.CustomerIDOrKey{Namespace: ns, IDOrKey: req.CustomerIDOrKey}}); if cus != nil && cus.IsDeleted() { return ..., models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError(...) }

patterns.name: Namespace resolved from context via namespaceDecoder
patterns.description: All handlers call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) first (delegating to h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx)); HTTP 500 if missing. Never accept namespace from URL params.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return def, err }

patterns.name: ParserV2.ToAPIGenericV2 for all entitlement responses
patterns.description: Handlers returning an entitlement call ParserV2.ToAPIGenericV2(ent, cust.ID, cust.Key), which dispatches by EntitlementType to ToMeteredV2/ToStaticV2/ToBooleanV2 and the corresponding FromEntitlement*V2 setter. Never inline the mapping.
patterns.example: v2, err := ParserV2.ToAPIGenericV2(ent, cus.ID, cus.Key); if err != nil { return api.EntitlementV2{}, err }; return *v2, nil

patterns.name: Error encoder chains v1 then generic
patterns.description: getErrorEncoder() composes entitlementdriver.GetErrorEncoder() (v1) with commonhttp.GenericErrorEncoder() so v2 error codes stay consistent with v1.

patterns.name: ParseAPICreateInputV2 for create/override input parsing
patterns.description: Mapping API create inputs uses ParseAPICreateInputV2(inp, ns, cus.GetUsageAttribution()), handling metered/static/boolean variants and enforcing issueAfterReset vs grants mutual exclusion. Never inline discriminator logic.
patterns.example: createInp, grantsInp, err := ParseAPICreateInputV2(request.APIInput, request.Namespace, cus.GetUsageAttribution())

patterns.name: Separate connector fields for base and metered ops
patterns.description: entitlementHandler holds connector (entitlement.Service) for generic CRUD and balanceConnector (meteredentitlement.Connector) for grant/history/reset; do not collapse them.

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementHandler interface listing all V2 methods plus entitlementHandler struct and NewEntitlementHandler constructor — sole DI entry point.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New handler methods must be added to the EntitlementHandler interface; a missing method breaks the compile-time contract.

key_file_guides.role: Customer-scoped CRUD entitlement handlers (create, list, get, delete, override), all using resolve-customer-first.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must always check cus.IsDeleted() before proceeding; omitting it lets soft-deleted customer operations succeed.

key_file_guides.role: Metered-specific handlers (grants CRUD, balance history, usage reset) routed through h.balanceConnector.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Resolves entitlement via h.connector.GetEntitlementOfCustomerAt before using ent.ID for balance calls — do not pass featureKey directly to balanceConnector.

key_file_guides.role: Namespace-level list and get-by-ID handlers (not customer-scoped); ListEntitlements uses ListEntitlementsWithCustomer to return customer context.
key_file_guides.watch_for: OrderBy values must pass through strcase.CamelToSnake before comparison against ListEntitlementsOrderBy.StrValues(); skipping causes silent invalid orderby acceptance.

key_file_guides.role: Domain-to-API conversions: parserV2 (ToAPIGenericV2/ToMeteredV2/ToStaticV2/ToBooleanV2), ParseAPICreateInputV2, MapEntitlementGrantToAPIV2, MapAPIGrantV2ToCreateGrantInput.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EntitlementV2 is a union — always use FromEntitlementMeteredV2/StaticV2/BooleanV2 to set the variant, not direct struct assignment.

key_file_guides.role: Single getErrorEncoder() composing v1 and generic error encoders.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not define new error types here; use models.Generic* errors or extend the v1 entitlementdriver encoder.

anti_patterns: Calling entitlement domain methods with a subject key string instead of first resolving the customer to cus.ID — v2 is customer-ID-based
Inlining entitlement type discrimination (switching on EntitlementType) in handlers instead of calling ParserV2.ToAPIGenericV2
Accepting namespace as a URL/query parameter rather than resolving via h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Skipping the cus.IsDeleted() check before any entitlement mutation
Adding business logic (grant burn-down, balance calculation) in handler or mapping files — delegate to connector/balanceConnector

decisions.decision: V2 driver is customer-ID-centric rather than subject-key-centric like v1.
decisions.rationale: The v2 API surface is customer-first; entitlements are always queried and returned with customerId/customerKey to align with the customer-scoped billing model.

decisions.decision: Error encoding delegates to v1 entitlementdriver.GetErrorEncoder() first.
decisions.rationale: Reusing v1 error codes prevents behavior divergence between API versions for the same domain errors.

decisions.decision: Customer resolution (including deleted check) happens in the operation closure, not deferred to the domain service.
decisions.rationale: Fail-fast on bad customer before hitting the entitlement service; the resolved cus also provides GetUsageAttribution() for building domain input.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new customer-scoped entitlement endpoint following the v2 pattern
